source: "national authorities", as defined in Article 2 of Regulation (EC) No 322/97, shall mean national statistical institutes and other bodies responsible in each Member State for producing Community statistics.
reference: "einzelstaatliche Stellen" gemäß der Definition in Artikel 2 der Verordnung (EG) Nr. 322/97 die nationalen statistischen Ämter und sonstige Einrichtungen, die in den einzelnen Mitgliedstaaten mit der Erstellung der Gemeinschaftsstatistiken beauftragt sind.
definitions_used: {'responsible': 'A person or body who can be held accountable for carrying out a duty is considered responsible.', 'regulation': 'A regulation is a rule made and maintained by an authority , typically a governmental agency , to control or govern conduct within its jurisdiction .', 'article': "An article is a legal rule that can be set out under a statute , a code , or a paragraph in a legal document. Articles usually refer to matters that have been agreed upon by the supervising authority. For example, in contracts , an article under the UCC can set out a list of contractual obligations between parties. It can regulate the parties' conduct, duties, expectations, measure of damages in breach cases, and how to resolve conflicts. Under the Constitution of the United States , there are seven articles which describe the way the government is structured and how it operates. These articles establish the relationship of the states in rapport to the Federal Government and hold that the Constitution is the supreme law of the land.", 'state': 'A state is a political division of a body of people that occupies a territory defined by frontiers. The state is sovereign in its territory (also referred to as jurisdiction ) and has the authority to enforce a system of rules over the people living inside it. That system of rules is commonly composed of a constitution , statutes , regulations , and common law .'}
translation: "nationale Behörden" im Sinne des Artikels 2 der Verordnung (EG) Nr. 322/97 bezeichnen die nationalen Statistikinstitute und andere für die Erstellung von Gemeinschaftsstatistiken in jedem Mitgliedstaat verantwortlichen Stellen.

source: Workers and unemployed workers whose families reside in a Member State other than the competent State

definitions_used: {'competent': 'The term competent is used in various legal contexts, including procedure , evidence , and employment . More generally, it refers to the ability to act in the circumstances, including the ability to perform a job or occupation, the capacity to reason, or to make decisions.', 'state': 'A state is a political division of a body of people that occupies a territory defined by frontiers. The state is sovereign in its territory (also referred to as jurisdiction ) and has the authority to enforce a system of rules over the people living inside it. That system of rules is commonly composed of a constitution , statutes , regulations , and common law .'}
translation: Arbeiter und arbeitslose Arbeiter, deren Familien in einem anderen Mitgliedstaat als dem zuständigen Staat wohnen

source: If a derogation was granted for an airport in year N but the airport changes category in year N, then the derogation is no longer valid for that year.
reference: Wurde einem Flughafen im Jahr N eine Ausnahmeregelung gewährt und wechselt dieser Flughafen im Jahr N die Klasse, dann verliert die Ausnahmeregelung für dieses Jahr ihre Gültigkeit.
translation: Wurde für einen Flughafen in Jahr N eine Abweichung gewährt, aber der Flughafen wechselt in Jahr N die Kategorie, dann ist die Abweichung für dieses Jahr nicht mehr gültig.

source: Member States shall transmit to the Commission a succinct yearly report on the implementation of this Regulation.
reference: Die Mitgliedstaaten übermitteln der Kommission jährlich einen Kurzbericht über die Anwendung dieser Verordnung.
definitions_used: {'commission': 'A commission is a fee or remuneration paid in return for services rendered. \xa0Commission is often calculated as a percentage of the total transaction ; a commission can be separate and in addition to fixed wages , or it can be the sole form of compensation , known as straight commission.', 'regulation': 'A regulation is a rule made and maintained by an authority , typically a governmental agency , to control or govern conduct within its jurisdiction .'}
translation: Die Mitgliedstaaten übermitteln der Kommission einen kurzen Jahresbericht über die Umsetzung dieser Verordnung.

source: All the abovementioned methods, taken from Council Directive 67/548/EEC, can also be found in the publication "Classification, Packaging and Labelling of Dangerous Substances in the European Union", Part 2: "Testing Methods".
reference: Alle vorstehend aufgeführten Methoden aus der Richtlinie 67/548/EWG des Rates sind ebenfalls zu finden in der Veröffentlichung "Einstufung, Verpackung und Kennzeichnung gefährlicher Stoffe in der Europäischen Union", Teil 2: "Prüfmethoden".
definitions_used: {'publication': 'A publication\xa0is\xa0the act of\xa0making something known to the public at large to inspect or scrutinize. This is done to\xa0convey knowledge or\xa0give notice .'}
translation: Alle vorerwähnten Methoden, die der Richtlinie 67/548/EWG des Rates entnommen sind, finden sich auch in der Veröffentlichung "Einstufung, Verpackung und Kennzeichnung gefährlicher Stoffe in der Europäischen Union", Teil 2: "Prüfmethoden".

source: Member States shall notify to the producers each new allocation or modification of their individual reference quantity by the means they consider the most appropriate, provided that it ensures that the producers have an effective knowledge of the reference quantity allocated.
reference: Die Mitgliedstaaten teilen den Erzeugern jede neue Zuteilung oder Änderung ihrer einzelbetrieblichen Referenzmenge auf die Art und Weise mit, die ihnen am besten geeignet erscheint, sofern sie gewährleistet, dass der Erzeuger tatsächlich über die zugeteilte Referenzmenge unterrichtet wird.
definitions_used: {'modification': 'In a legal context, the term "modification" generally refers to the act of changing, altering, or amending something that is already established, such as a contract , statute , court order , or legal document. It involves making revisions or adjustments to the existing terms , provisions, conditions , or language.'}
translation: Die Mitgliedstaaten teilen den Erzeugern jede neue Zuweisung oder Änderung ihrer individuellen Referenzmenge mit den Mitteln mit, die sie für am geeignetsten erachten, vorausgesetzt, dass dadurch sichergestellt ist, dass die Erzeuger effektives Wissen über die zugeteilte Referenzmenge haben.

source: The group shall adopt its own rules of procedure on the basis of proposals made by the Commission.
reference: Die Gruppe gibt sich auf Vorschlag der Kommission eine Geschäftsordnung.
definitions_used: {'commission': 'A commission is a fee or remuneration paid in return for services rendered. \xa0Commission is often calculated as a percentage of the total transaction ; a commission can be separate and in addition to fixed wages , or it can be the sole form of compensation , known as straight commission.', 'procedure': 'See procedural law .', 'adopt': 'To adopt is to accept, to bring, to choose to take up, to follow, or to use a plan or technique. To adopt is to assume a report or position, attitude, or law, as to apply them.', 'basis': "Basis is the value assigned to an asset , usually its purchase cost, and reflects the owner's investment. It generally includes the amount paid in cash, debt , services, or property, along with related costs such as commissions , taxes , transportation, and legal or transfer fees. This value may increase due to improvements or decrease through deductions like depreciation , resulting in an adjusted basis over time.", 'rules': 'In general, rules are any standard, principle, or norm that guides conduct. Thus, any factual situation calling for a decision might be thought of as raising two questions: first, what rules apply to these facts; second, what decision should be made if those rules are to be followed?'}
translation: Die Gruppe gibt sich ihre eigene Geschäftsordnung auf der Grundlage von Vorschlägen der Kommission.

source: "This permit is issued pursuant to Regulation (EEC) No 1612/68 of the Council of the European Communities of 15 October 1968 and to the measures taken in implementation of the Council Directive of 15 October 1968.
reference: "Diese Aufenthaltserlaubnis wird auf Grund der Verordnung (EWG) Nr. 1612/68 des Rates der Europäischen Gemeinschaften vom 15. Oktober 1968 und der zur Durchführung der Richtlinie des Rates vom 15. Oktober 1968 erlassenen Vorschriften ausgestellt.
definitions_used: {'regulation': 'A regulation is a rule made and maintained by an authority , typically a governmental agency , to control or govern conduct within its jurisdiction .', 'permit': 'A permit, sometimes referred to as a license , is an official written statement that someone is allowed to do something. A permit is usually granted by a state or city in return for a fee . For example, a firearms permit or license allows the holder of the permit to buy, own, possess , or carry a firearm subject to conditions.'}
translation: Diese Erlaubnis wird gemäß der Verordnung (EWG) Nr. 1612/68 des Rates der Europäischen Gemeinschaften vom 15. Oktober 1968 und den Maßnahmen, die zur Durchführung der Richtlinie des Rates vom 15. Oktober 1968 getroffen wurden, erteilt.

source: Having regard to Regulation (EC) No 999/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 22 May 2001 laying down rules for the prevention, control and eradication of certain transmissible spongiform encephalopathies(1), and in particular the first paragraph of Article 23 thereof,

definitions_used: {'regulation': 'A regulation is a rule made and maintained by an authority , typically a governmental agency , to control or govern conduct within its jurisdiction .', 'article': "An article is a legal rule that can be set out under a statute , a code , or a paragraph in a legal document. Articles usually refer to matters that have been agreed upon by the supervising authority. For example, in contracts , an article under the UCC can set out a list of contractual obligations between parties. It can regulate the parties' conduct, duties, expectations, measure of damages in breach cases, and how to resolve conflicts. Under the Constitution of the United States , there are seven articles which describe the way the government is structured and how it operates. These articles establish the relationship of the states in rapport to the Federal Government and hold that the Constitution is the supreme law of the land.", 'control': 'Control as a verb is to exercise one’s power or authority over something or someone, to regulate or govern, or to have a controlling interest in. Control as a noun is the power to govern, manage, direct, or oversee something or someone.', 'rules': 'In general, rules are any standard, principle, or norm that guides conduct. Thus, any factual situation calling for a decision might be thought of as raising two questions: first, what rules apply to these facts; second, what decision should be made if those rules are to be followed?', 'may': 'The word “may” is an expression of possibility, a permissive choice to act or not, and ordinarily implies some degree of discretion . This contrasts with the word “ shall ,” which is generally used to indicate a mandatory provision. As explained in this case from Texas , the word “may” in a written instrument is generally construed as permissive, but it can be interpreted as mandatory depending on the context in which it is used. Usually, only where it is necessary to give effect to the clear intention of the legislature that it can be construed in a mandatory sense.'}
